name: F A B Church
description_short: F A B Church Federal Credit Union is a church-based credit union in Savannah, GA, serving First African Baptist Church members and families since 1954.
description_long: F A B Church Federal Credit Union was established in May 1954 by First African Baptist Church in Savannah, Georgia, making it the first federal credit union housed within a church. For over 70 years, it has served the local community with financial products tailored to members' needs. The credit union operates with a faith-based community focus, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional banks by aligning financial services with spiritual values. The credit union offers savings accounts, personal loans (starting at 6.00% APR), holiday loans, vacation loans, financial counseling, financial education, student scholarships, retirement planning, and online and mobile banking services. Members can apply for loans online, join through their application portal, or visit in person at their single location in Savannah. What distinguishes F A B Church FCU is its unique positioning as a church-based credit union with membership restricted to First African Baptist Church members and their immediate family. This creates a tight-knit community focus rather than serving the general public. The organization emphasizes aligning financial solutions with members' spiritual values and community needs, offering scholarship programs and financial counseling alongside standard credit union products. However, F A B Church FCU has significant operational limitations that restrict its utility. With hours limited to Tuesday and Thursday 12-2pm, and Sunday 9-9:45am (one hour after service), accessing services is extremely difficult for working members. The single location at 23 Montgomery St in Savannah means no branch accessibility outside the immediate area. Membership is restricted to a specific church community, limiting potential growth and access. No information is provided about savings account rates, CDs, or other deposit products beyond mentioning high-yield savings accounts.
pros: Low personal loan rates starting at 6.00% APR as of July 2024
Church-based community focus aligns financial services with spiritual values
Offers financial counseling and financial education to members
Provides student scholarships as member benefit
Online and mobile banking available for convenience
Long operational history since 1954 demonstrates stability
Membership restricted to create tight-knit community support
cons: Extremely limited hours (Tuesday-Thursday 12-2pm, Sunday 9-9:45am only) make access difficult for working members
Single physical location in Savannah with no branches or ATM network mentioned
Membership restricted to First African Baptist Church members and immediate family only—not open to general public
No information provided about savings account rates, CD rates, or other deposit product yields
Limited contact options (phone, email, in-person only during restricted hours)
best_for: First African Baptist Church members and their immediate family seeking affordable personal loans
Community members prioritizing faith-aligned financial services over traditional bank relationships
Savannah-area residents with flexible schedules who can visit during limited operating hours
Members interested in financial education, counseling, and student scholarship opportunities
diagnosis: F A B Church FCU is best for First African Baptist Church members in Savannah seeking competitive personal loan rates within a faith-based community context. The main caveats are severely restricted operating hours (only 7 hours per week), membership limited to one specific church and families, and no information about deposit product rates or broader financial services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
